mission.title: fluentd: Memory leak
mission.description: Memory leak. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluentd analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluentd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluentd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd
```
I noticed a memory issue, what looks to me as a leak. I had a more complex configuration, but boiled it down to this simple config just to rule out the specifics in my config. Even with this (see below) config, I observe the memory leakage.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Memory leak
steps.description:
I've confirmed the issue.
I think it's not fluentd's issue, it's fluent-plugin-systemd's issue.
Please forward the report to https://github.com/fluent-plugin-systemd/fluent-plugin-systemd

Details:

* I cannot reproduce it by replacing `<source>` with other plugins like in_tail (with tailing a huge
```yaml
Memory leak:
```

steps.title: Confirm Memory leak is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I've confirmed the issue.
I think it's not fluentd's issue, it's fluent-plugin-systemd's issue.
Please forward the report to https://github.com/fluent-plugin-systemd/fluent-plugin-systemd

Details:

* I cannot reproduce it by replacing `<source>` with other plugins like in_tail (with tailing a huge file).
* The issue is caused by `read_from_head` of in_systemd.
codeSnippets:
When you use jemalloc, `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX` doesn't make sense, so you can remove it.
I face memory leak as well
I use input: 

- `forward`
- `http` for the liveness and readiness probes
- `systemd`

output:

- `null` for fluent logs
- `opensearch` with **file buffer**
- `stdout` for health checks.

The file buffer is always flushed but memory is getting increased.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51335355/171393052-7eab1730-5e53-4eea-b835-c9d0f81531a4.png)

Version:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluentd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
